Treat consent for evaluation as automatic placement consent.
Skip notice when the team expects eligibility.
knowledgeChecks.rationale: IDEA distinguishes informed consent for initial evaluation from consent for initial provision of services; current jurisdictional procedures must be followed.
knowledgeChecks.reviewNote: Independently authored and source reviewed for study use; independent school-psychologist validation remains pending.

knowledgeChecks.prompt: A staff member requests a full psychological record without a stated educational need. What should the psychologist do?
choices: Share because all employees have equal access.
Apply FERPA, current law and policy, consent and legitimate-interest rules, and disclose only authorized necessary information securely.
Post a summary in a shared channel.
Refuse all disclosure even during a lawful emergency exception.
knowledgeChecks.rationale: School employment does not create unlimited access; privacy decisions require authority, purpose, minimum necessary information, and documentation.
knowledgeChecks.reviewNote: Independently authored and source reviewed for study use; independent school-psychologist validation remains pending.

knowledgeChecks.prompt: What does IDEA require of evaluation procedures?
choices: Use one IQ score as the sole criterion.
Assess only the suspected label area.
Use varied, technically sound, nondiscriminatory tools administered appropriately and never rely on one measure as the sole criterion.
Use English measures regardless of native language.
knowledgeChecks.rationale: IDEA requires comprehensive, relevant, nondiscriminatory evaluation using multiple tools and sources.
knowledgeChecks.reviewNote: Independently authored and source reviewed for study use; independent school-psychologist validation remains pending.

knowledgeChecks.prompt: A family disagrees with the district evaluation and requests an independent educational evaluation at public expense. What must the agency generally do?
choices: Require the family to prove why it disagrees before responding.
Ignore the request until the next reevaluation.
Choose an examiner based on criteria that defeat the right.
Without unnecessary delay, provide the IEE at public expense or file due process to defend the agency evaluation, subject to governing criteria.
knowledgeChecks.rationale: IDEA provides a specific IEE process; the agency may ask but cannot require an explanation or unreasonably delay its response.
knowledgeChecks.reviewNote: Independently authored and source reviewed for study use; independent school-psychologist validation remains pending.

knowledgeChecks.prompt: A student has a disability that substantially limits a major life activity but does not need special education. What should the team consider?
choices: An individualized Section 504 evaluation and plan for equal access, using current procedures rather than an automatic diagnosis-based package.
IDEA eligibility is the only disability protection.
Every diagnosis requires the same accommodation.
A psychologist may create the plan unilaterally.
knowledgeChecks.rationale: Section 504 is a civil-rights protection that can require individualized aids or services even without IDEA eligibility.
knowledgeChecks.reviewNote: Independently authored and source reviewed for study use; independent school-psychologist validation remains pending.

memoryAids.title: RIOT Before a Routine Battery
memoryAids.content: Review records, Interview, Observe, and Test or otherwise directly measure only what answers the referral question. Multiple relevant methods beat automatic test batteries.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Problem Solving: Define, Analyze, Act, Evaluate
memoryAids.content: Operationalize the gap, test alterable hypotheses, implement a matched strategy, and evaluate outcome plus fidelity using an advance decision rule.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Score Range, Not Score Point
memoryAids.content: Use reliability, standard error, confidence intervals, norm relevance, and context. One obtained score is not a perfectly precise trait value.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Reliable Does Not Mean Valid for This Use
memoryAids.content: Consistency is necessary but not sufficient. Ask whether evidence supports the exact construct interpretation and decision for this student and context.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Consultation: Relationship, Problem, Plan, Review
memoryAids.content: Build trust, define the shared measurable concern, create a feasible role-clear plan, then review fidelity, outcomes, acceptability, and next action.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Instruction: Model, Guide, Feedback, Release
memoryAids.content: Explicitly model the target, guide many responses, give immediate feedback and review, then release independence only when learner data show readiness.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Access Preserves the Construct
memoryAids.content: An accommodation removes a barrier unrelated to the goal. A modification changes content or expected performance. Document any effect on interpretation.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Tiers Describe Support, Not Students
memoryAids.content: Tier 1 is universal, Tier 2 targeted, and Tier 3 intensive. Students move among support intensities based on need and response; tiers are not identities.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: FBA: Context, Antecedent, Behavior, Consequence, Function
memoryAids.content: Define the response, compare occurrences and nonoccurrences, build a testable function, then prevent, teach, and reinforce a matched replacement response.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Safety First, No Secrecy Promise
memoryAids.content: For imminent harm, maintain supervision and activate current crisis procedures. For suspected abuse, follow current reporting law without investigating independently.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: MTSS Integrates, It Does Not Delay
memoryAids.content: Coordinate academic, behavior, and mental-health screening and supports through common problem solving; never use intervention tiers to delay a required evaluation.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Climate Needs Perception and Outcome Data
memoryAids.content: Combine student, family, and staff voice with attendance, discipline, learning, referral, observation, and subgroup evidence. Silence is not proof of safety.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Bullying Is Not Balanced Conflict
memoryAids.content: Power imbalance makes equal compromise unsafe. Protect, investigate through authorized channels, support, hold accountable, prevent systemically, and monitor.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Threat Assessment Is Facts, Not Profiles
memoryAids.content: Use a trained multidisciplinary process to investigate context, intent, capability, stressors, protective factors, needs, management, and follow-up.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Family Partnership: Access Plus Influence
memoryAids.content: Remove schedule, language, disability, technology, trust, and transportation barriers, then ensure family evidence and priorities can actually shape decisions.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Bias Audit the Decision Path
memoryAids.content: Examine operational definitions and disaggregated rates at referral, discipline, screening, testing, eligibility, placement, and service access—not final counts alone.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Research: Claim Sets the Design
memoryAids.content: Causal, descriptive, predictive, experience, and implementation questions need different designs. Match methods to the claim and state alternative explanations.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Significant Is Not Necessarily Important
memoryAids.content: After a p value, inspect effect size, confidence interval, practical benefit, cost, adverse effects, subgroup response, validity, and replication.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: IDEA Evaluation: Multiple, Relevant, Accessible, Comprehensive
memoryAids.content: Use varied technically sound sources, appropriate language and access, all suspected areas, parent evidence, and no single sole criterion.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.

memoryAids.title: Ethics: Define, Standards, Consult, Options, Act, Review
memoryAids.content: Name the dilemma and power, review current ethics and law, consult appropriately, compare consequences, document action, and evaluate the result.
memoryAids.reviewNote: Retrieval cue only; apply the underlying concept and verify current law, ethics, standards, crisis procedures, and district requirements.
